The '''Skirmish near Gelgelar''' was a space battle between [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces and elements of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] that took place shortly after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] seized control of the [[Gelgelar system/Legends|Gelgelar system]]. The skirmish erupted when a deep-space operation to transfer survivors of the [[Evacuation of Gelgelar]] to the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|medical frigate]] [[Redemption/Legends|''Redemption'']] was interrupted by the arrival of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Nemesis]]'' and a minor force of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]].

==The Battle==
The [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] believed that the transfer of wounded personnel from the abandoned Gelgelar base to the ''Redemption'' would be discreet and avoid Imperial detection. While the operation started out smoothly for the Rebels, as both transports carrying medical supplies successfully docked with the ''Redemption'', the Star Destroyer ''Nemesis'' dropped out of hyperspace and deployed its fighters soon after the first [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|Gallofree medium transport]] carrying wounded docked with the medical frigate.

[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue]] and [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadrons]] were hard pressed to fend off the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] in their [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95]] craft, but the outclassed snubfighters somehow managed to keep the Imperials at bay. With the fighter complement of the ''Nemesis'' virtually depleted and the nimble Headhunters evading the Star Destroyer's [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, the Imperials called in reinforcements in form of several [[Flight group/Legends|flight groups]] of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]], putting even more pressure on the Rebel fighters to prevent damage to their ships.

After roughly fifteen minutes of intense fighting, the last transport departed from the ''Redemption'', but it was destroyed by the ''Nemesis'', which had finally moved into firing range. The ''Redemption'', all batteries firing, took a massive beating from the Star Destroyer's turbolasers, but the frigate's shields held long enough to allow it to escape into hyperspace, along with its fighter escort.

==Aftermath==
Thanks to the transfer from the GR75 medium transports to the ''Redemption'', many Rebel lives were likely saved by the medical facilities aboard the frigate.

With the dust from the First Battle of Gelgelar settling, Rebel High Command saw a need for a new base of operations in the [[Airam sector/Legends|Airam sector]] now that [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]] had been overrun by [[Task Force Vengeance]]. The Alliance made a decision to deploy additional forces there.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]'' {{1st}}
